children.name: Remiz
children.description: The penduline tits — tiny, acrobatic passerines named for their remarkable hanging nests, woven from plant fibres and spider silk into a pouch suspended from the tip of a thin branch. The Eurasian Penduline Tit weaves an elaborate flask-shaped nest with a false entrance and a hidden side chamber for the eggs. Males build multiple nests and females select the best one.

children.name: Remiz pendulinus
children.description: The best-known penduline tit, found across Europe and Asia. Chestnut-brown back, grey head with a black mask, and a delicate pointed bill. The male's nest is an architectural marvel — a felted pouch of plant down, willow catkin fibres, and spider silk, suspended from a twig tip over water. Nests near reeds and marshes across southern Sweden.

children.name: Remiz coronatus
children.description: Ranges from Central Asia through Kazakhstan and Mongolia to northern China and Pakistan. Distinguished by a white crown contrasting with a black mask. Inhabits riverside woodland and reedbeds. Builds the same elaborate hanging nest as its Eurasian relative.

children.name: Remiz consobrinus
children.description: Breeds in northeastern China, Korea, and adjacent Russia. Winters in eastern China and Japan. Greyer than the Eurasian Penduline Tit with less chestnut on the back. Formerly treated as a subspecies of R. pendulinus.

children.name: Anthoscopus
children.description: African penduline tits — tiny, mouse-grey birds of savanna and woodland. They construct the most elaborate nests of any bird: a felted pouch with a false entrance that the bird must push open from below. The entrance is so well-camouflaged that predators cannot find it. Six species across sub-Saharan Africa.

children.name: Anthoscopus minutus
children.description: A tiny, pale grey bird of southern African savanna and scrub. Weighs only 6-8 grams. Its nest is a masterpiece of deception — a soft pouch with a hidden slit entrance that the bird opens by pushing through, leaving no visible opening. The male and female cooperate in building and raising the young.

children.name: Anthoscopus paradoxus
children.description: Found in northeastern Africa from Ethiopia to Tanzania. Greenish-yellow plumage, unlike the greyer southern species. Inhabits dry acacia woodland and thornbush. Its nest, like other penduline tits, features a cunning false entrance.

children.name: Anthoscopus musculus
children.description: A tiny penduline tit from East Africa.

children.name: Anthoscopus sylviella
children.description: A penduline tit from Central Africa.

children.name: Anthoscopus caroli
children.description: A penduline tit found across southern Africa.

children.name: Anthoscopus paradoxus
children.description: A yellow penduline tit from northeastern Africa.

children.name: Anthoscopus musculus
children.description: A tiny penduline tit from East Africa.

children.name: Anthoscopus caroli
children.description: A penduline tit from southern Africa.
